location.office_time_required: up to one day per week from home (primarily in-office)

seniority.level: Junior / Early Career (Associate Analyst, structured program)
level_signals: Associate Analyst program is a three-to-five-year structured program
Designed to develop future fundamental investment analysts
Exceptional performers have the potential to be promoted to Investment Analyst
Base salary $110,000-$160,000 consistent with junior analyst range at asset managers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's Degree with a strong academic record required
Strong Excel and financial modelling skills including previous experience building three-statement models

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong academic background and intellectual curiosity
Highly motivated, confident self-starter, with ability to work independently and collaboratively in a performance-driven environment
A demonstrated interest and passion for equity investing and financial markets
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Demonstrated ability to derive meaningful insights from in-depth research and analysis
Preference for candidates who have made progress toward the CFA designation
Familiarity with LLMs, Claude Code, and/or Python is preferred

explicitly_excluded: FINRA licenses are not required and will not be supported for this role.

Applicants for employment in the US must have work authorization that does not now or in the future require sponsorship of a visa for employment authorization in the United States (e.g., H1-B visa, F-1 visa (OPT), TN visa or any other non-immigrant work status).

scope.surface: Large-Cap Growth Strategy — fundamental equity research support for Portfolio Manager
scope.business_model: B2B and B2C asset management (institutional, retirement, individual clients)
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality of investment research and recommendations
core_responsibilities: Provide fundamental research support to Large-Cap Growth Strategy Portfolio Manager
Collaboration with investment analysts and portfolio managers
Extensive financial analysis and company/industry related research projects
Incorporate macroeconomic and market analysis into bottom-up company research
Interact with public company C-suite executives
Learn directly from senior investment professionals
Provide investment insight and impact the investment process beyond just number crunching

green_flags: Base salary range disclosed ($110,000-$160,000) — consistent and transparent across all locations
Structured 3-to-5-year development program with mentoring and training
Clear promotion path from Associate Analyst to Investment Analyst for exceptional performers
Access to senior investment professionals and C-suite executives directly
Competitive compensation with discretionary bonus upside
Established firm with 85+ years of asset management experience
red_flags: Hybrid policy is heavily in-office — only one day per week from home; fully remote candidates ineligible
No visa sponsorship under any circumstances; work authorization requirement is strict
JD city in URL is Baltimore, MD but location is not explicitly named in body — primary city inferred from URL
Most 'Preferred' qualifications read like de facto expectations for a competitive candidate pool at a top asset manager

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Associate Analyst' program description, structured duration, and comp range)
location.cities_listed (Baltimore, MD inferred from URL; body says 'our offices' generically)
